title: ds-viz-parallelcoordinates - option matrix (light)
description: Light-theme companion. Test bench for splunk.parallelcoordinates. Verifies all options from the 10.4 PDF: backgroundColor, lineColor, lineOpacity (0-1 or %), showNullAxis. Demonstrates field-order semantics (each column = one axis), null-axis behavior, and styling for high vs low cardinality.

ds_perf_4axis.name: service x cpu x mem x latency x errors - 4 numeric axes

options.query: | makeresults count=24 | streamstats count AS rn | eval service = case(rn%4==0,"checkout", rn%4==1,"catalog", rn%4==2,"payments", 1==1,"search") | eval cpu_pct = round(20 + random()%70) | eval mem_gb = round(2 + random()%14) | eval latency_ms = round(50 + random()%450) | eval errors = round(random()%30) | table service cpu_pct mem_gb latency_ms errors

ds_nutrients.name: canonical PDF example - food groups x calories x protein x water

options.query: | makeresults count=18 | streamstats count AS rn | eval nutrients_group = case(rn%6==0,"Vegetables", rn%6==1,"Fruits", rn%6==2,"Grains", rn%6==3,"Dairy", rn%6==4,"Meats", 1==1,"Sweets") | eval nutrients_calories = round(30 + random()%500) | eval protein_g = round(random()%40) | eval water_g = round(40 + random()%55) | table nutrients_group nutrients_calories protein_g water_g

ds_with_nulls.name: data with nulls - to test showNullAxis

options.query: | makeresults count=20 | streamstats count AS rn | eval product = case(rn%4==0,"sku-A", rn%4==1,"sku-B", rn%4==2,"sku-C", 1==1,"sku-D") | eval revenue = round(100 + random()%900) | eval cost = if(rn%5==0, null(), round(40 + random()%500)) | eval margin = if(isnotnull(cost), revenue - cost, null()) | eval units = round(10 + random()%200) | table product revenue cost margin units

ds_correlation.name: axes with strong correlation - shows visual lines collapse

options.query: | makeresults count=30 | streamstats count AS rn | eval x = round(20 + random()%80) | eval correlated = round(x * 1.2 + random()%10) | eval inverse = round(120 - x + random()%5) | eval noise = round(random()%100) | eval cohort = case(rn%3==0,"alpha", rn%3==1,"beta", 1==1,"gamma") | table cohort x correlated inverse noise

options.markdown:
# `splunk.parallelcoordinates` - option matrix (light)

Parallel coordinates plot **multiple numeric variables on parallel vertical axes**, one row of data drawn as a line that threads through every axis. Best for spotting clusters, correlations, and outliers across 4-8 dimensions.

Full options list from the **10.4 PDF**:
- `backgroundColor`
- `lineColor`
- `lineOpacity` (0-1 or %)
- `showNullAxis` (default `true`)

The field order in your SPL **defines the axes** - the first column becomes axis 1, the second becomes axis 2, etc.

viz_default.title: 1. Default - 5 axes, brand purple
viz_default.description: Default lineColor #7B56DB, lineOpacity 0.5. Field order in SPL controls axis order.

viz_canonical.title: 2. Canonical PDF example
viz_canonical.description: nutrients_group x nutrients_calories x protein_g x water_g - same shape as PDF.

viz_low_opacity.title: 3. lineOpacity=0.15
viz_low_opacity.description: Useful when many lines overlap. Density becomes the visual cue.

viz_high_opacity.title: 4. lineOpacity=0.95
viz_high_opacity.description: Use only when you have <20 rows; otherwise lines crush each other.

viz_show_null.title: 7. showNullAxis=true (default)
viz_show_null.description: Rows with null values still render. Lines drop to a separate 'null' tick on the affected axis.

viz_hide_null.title: 8. showNullAxis=false
viz_hide_null.description: Cleaner axes. Rows with nulls in any displayed field are suppressed visually.

viz_correlation.title: 9. Correlation - parallel = correlated, X = inverse
viz_correlation.description: axes go: x, correlated, inverse, noise. Lines visually parallel between x/correlated and form an X between x/inverse.

viz_tinted_background.title: 10. Custom backgroundColor
viz_tinted_background.description: Custom background paired with brand line color.

viz_pct_opacity.title: 11. lineOpacity as a percentage string
viz_pct_opacity.description: Per the PDF, both `0.50` and `"50%"` are valid forms.

viz_dense.title: 12. Dense data - the density story
viz_dense.description: Combine many rows + low opacity to reveal modal paths.
